response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, C++) and strong software craftsmanship habits (TDD, CI/CD, testing), but the resume shows no substantive Kotlin or Spring ecosystem work, no IDE/language-tooling background, and only peripheral Java exposure from 2008-2011 on robotic arms and a mobile orchestration tool. The role demands deep JVM expertise, Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate fluency, and genuine interest in IDE internals — none of which are evidenced here. Recent work centers on TypeScript product engineering and AI agent tooling, which is a poor match for an enterprise JVM IDE position.
strengths: Long track record on complex systems and large codebases across multiple domains
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, refactoring dojos, custom test runners and assertion libraries
Experience building developer tools and frameworks (MCP code generators, Trial, Fluent-Asserts)
concerns: No demonstrated Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience
Java exposure is dated and shallow; Kotlin not mentioned at all
No IDE internals, language tooling, or JVM-deep work in the employment history
